channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Alleged N8.83trn in unbudgeted expenditure: Tinubu must resign –Peter Obi
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: For the second time in less than one month, Mr. Peter Obi, former Anambra State governor and presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), has asked President Bola Tinubu to resign citing an alleged N8.83 trillion in unbudgeted expenditure flagged in a recent International Moneta

item.title: LPDC hears misconduct petition against SAN, 2 others July 29
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Legal Practitioners Disciplinary Committee (LPDC) has adjourned until July 29 the hearing of a petition alleging professional misconduct against Mr Ikechukwu Ezechukwu, SAN, and two other lawyers. The post LPDC hears misconduct petition against SAN, 2 others July 29 appeared first on The Sun Nig

item.title: EU awards 89 Nigerians fully funded master’s scholarships
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The European Union (EU) has awarded 89 Nigerians fully funded Erasmus Mundus Joint Master’s scholarships for the 2026 academic cycle, following Nigeria’s retention of its position as Africa’s leading beneficiary of the prestigious international scholarship programme. The post EU awards 89 Nigerians

item.title: Women empowerment programme strategic investment in Kano’s economic future –Gov Yusuf
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Kano State Governor, Abba Kabir Yusuf, has reaffirmed his administration’s commitment to empowering women economically, describing the state’s monthly women empowerment programme as a deliberate investment aimed at building a stronger and more prosperous Kano. The post Women empowerment programme st

item.title: India Stocks Shine as Foreigners Seek Defense Against AI Storm
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A stronger macro backdrop and optimism ahead of the earnings season are adding to the appeal.

item.title: FG begins rebuilding Borno trans-border highway to boost trade
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government has started reconstruction of trans-border highway in Borno to boost trade with neighbouring African countries and improve effectiveness of military operations against terrorists and insurgents around Lake Chad. The post FG begins rebuilding Borno trans-border highway to boost

item.title: NYSC reforms’ll undermine national defence policy –Lawmaker
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Member of the House of Representatives, representing Ado/Okpokwu/Ogbadibo Federal Constituency of Benue State, Philip Agbese, has said recent reforms to the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) approved by the Federal Government could erode the objectives of the scheme and undermine the country’s def

item.title: SERAP demands NASS’ probe ₦1.3bn allocation to ‘fictitious’ presidential council
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) has called on the leadership of the National Assembly to disclose all records relating to the allocation of over ₦1.3 billion to the alleged Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC)/Presidential Economic Advisory Council

item.title: Seplat ends gas flaring in its onshore operations, begins LPG production
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Seplat Energy Plc said it has ended routine gas flaring across its onshore operations in Nigeria, describing the milestone as a major step in its commitment to cleaner energy and environmental sustainability. The post Seplat ends gas flaring in its onshore operations, begins LPG production appeared

item.title: Why insecurity is worsening in Nigeria, First African Church Primate, Matilukuro
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Primate of The First African Church Mission (Worldwide), His Eminence, The Most Rev. Sunday Oludare Matilukuro, has attributed the worsening insecurity in Nigeria to the actvities of a few selfish compatriots arming and protecting the bandits. The post Why insecurity is worsening in Nigeria, First

item.title: Uzodimma lauds Tinubu, NASS over state police bill
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Imo State Governor and Chairman of the Progressive Governors Forum (PGF), Senator Hope Uzodimma, has commended President Bola Ahmed Tinubu and the National Assembly for advancing the constitutional process towards the establishment of state police, describing the development as a landmark step in ta

item.title: Alleged extortion: FUOYE deputy dean claims suspension pre-planned
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A suspended FUOYE Deputy Dean claims his suspension over alleged student extortion was pre-planned, citing a long-standing attempt to remove him. Read more
Read More: https://punchng.com/alleged-extortion-fuoye-deputy-dean-claims-suspension-pre-planned/

item.title: Kwara’ndupe rally and the politics of 2027, By Hassan Kabir Olayinka
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Political rallies are often dismissed as spectacles that bear little relationship to election outcomes. History has shown that large crowds do not necessarily translate into votes, just as modest gatherings do not always signal electoral weakness. Yet, there are moments when a political event become

item.title: Sports stadium becomes home for victims of the Venezuela earthquakes
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: A sports stadium in La Guaira state has been turned into a makeshift home and logistics centre for thousands of victims.

item.title: US Beef Exports to China to Pick Up as Rivals Exhaust Quotas
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China’s decision to renew import licenses for hundreds of US meat plants is yet to revive the beef trade, but the odds of more shipments in the second half of the year are improving.

item.title: SK Hynix Seeks Valuation Boost in $29 Billion US Listing
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: SK Hynix Inc.'s $29 billion US stock-market listing may help the company compete in the global stock market for memory chips used in AI computing. Bloomberg's Sangmi Cha breaks down the strategy. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Norway Stuns Brazil With Two Late Goals to Knockout 5-time Champions from 2026 World Cup

item.description: Duro Ikhazuagbe  Brazil’s quests to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup and add a sixth star to their shirts ended last night with Manchester City striker, Erling Haaland, scored  two late

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Nigeria’s D’Tigers made a loud statement in Luanda, Angola yesterday that they mean business as they delivered a blowout win over Rwanda, 62-106. Playing the men from Kigali in the

item.title: Experts seek more details on World Bank’s new Nigeria partnership framework
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: ECONOMIC analysts and development stakeholders have called for greater clarity on the implementation of the World Bank Group’s newly unveiled Country Partnership Framework (CPF) for Nigeria, following the announcement of a $1.25 billion financing package aimed at supporting private sector-led growth

item.description: 'I will finish when I choose,' Ronaldo reveals amid speculation about his World Cup future ahead of the last-16 match.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Minister of Works, Sen. Dave Umahi, has blamed the flooding of the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway on non-implementation of a Lagos State master plan with regard to flooding. Umahi also blamed the flooding on indiscriminate dumping of refuse by Lagos State residents. Umahi made the blame during an

item.dc:creator: Vanguard

item.title: Tankers Cross Hormuz Via US-Protected Corridor
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Western navies continue to say that the threat risk in the Strait of Hormuz is substantial and that the center of the strait has been mined, as ships try to avoid the attentions of Iran's military. Bloomberg's Michael Heath reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

While the initiative promotes entrepreneurship and self-reliance, many Nigerians view

item.title: National Security: Beyond the Headlines
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By CHIDO ONUMAH On July 2, 2026, Nigeria’s defence, intelligence, law enforcement, anti-corruption, emergency response, and public safety agencies took an extraordinary step by jointly addressing the nation on the state of national security. The initiative emerged from consultations among the leader

item.title: India Banks Slash Short-Term Debt Sales on Cheaper Forex Funding
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Indian lenders are pulling back short-term debt sales as the central bank’s recent push to attract foreign-currency deposits has opened up a cheaper and more durable source of funding.

item.title: Towards a realistic, sustainable minimum wage
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Federal Government is right to reopen the minimum-wage question, because no wage can be called fair if it is continually outrun by inflation and the rising cost of survival. The N70,000 minimum wage was agreed in 2024 at the outset of President Bola Tinubu’s economic reforms. Nigerians were aske

item.title: The man no-one wants to face - Haaland fires Norway to history
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Erling Haaland has scored seven goals in four World Cup games and he is the striker that nobody wants to face as he fires Norway into an historic quarter-final.

item.title: State Police: Separating facts from fictions
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By OPEYEMI BAMIDELE Nigeria, the country of our collective heritage, is again at a point of inflection. This is a reality that viciously confronts this federation of six geo-political zones, 36 states and 774 local government areas with a population of 242.46 million. We all have the duty to confron

item.title: When uneducated minds change the education system, by Owei Lakemfa
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Federal Government has announced it is replacing the 6-3-3-4 education system with a linear 12-year one. This is without wide consultations with the citizenry or the major stakeholders like parents, intellectuals, teachers, students, employers and labour. Just nine months ago, government had, wi

item.title: Ageing Brazil need major surgery - but is Ancelotti the man to do it?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: There was nothing unlucky about Brazil's loss to Norway in the last 16 of the World Cup and their exit at this stage is an unmitigated disaster, writes Tim Vickery.

item.title: Lagos pilots 80m clean cookstoves project to unlock carbon market
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: NIGERIA has commenced the pilot phase of an 80 million clean cookstoves initiative, a project expected to reshape household energy consumption, unlock billions of naira in climate finance and position the country as a major player in the global carbon market. The programme, spearheaded by Greenplint

item.title: Court halts Lagos takeover of widow’s estate
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Lagos State High Court has restrained the Lagos State Government from taking over an 80-year-old widow’s estate in Lekki, pending further hearing.

item.description:

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Asthma is a common respiratory condition that affects millions of people across the world, including Nigerians. Despite its prevalence, there is still a lack of understanding about the condition. Many people do not recognise its symptoms or know how to respond when someone is struggling to breathe o

item.title: Worsening hunger crisis in the north
item.dc:creator: The Sun Editorial
item.description: The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) has warned that Nigeria’s food security crisis is deteriorating faster than previously anticipated. The UN agency also explained that conflict is driving hunger in some northern states, especially the North-East, to levels not seen in a decade. According

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The ringgit is poised for a rebound after ending June as Asia’s worst performer, with measures to boost foreign-exchange inflows and strong economic fundamentals expected to support the recovery, analysts say.

item.title: Bankers Say Asia Loan Market to Stay Weak as War Saps Confidence
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Asia’s loan market is heading into the second half of the year with little sign of a rebound, according to bankers, as the fallout from the Iran war continues to suppress confidence among lenders and borrowers.

item.description: Chris Gotterup produced a weekend-best 62 - including nine birdies- in his final round to claim the John Deere Classic title, finishing 20 under in a thriller in Illinois.

item.title: World Cup: Haaland eliminates Brazil, fires Norway into historic quarter-finals
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Norway will now face the winners of the Round of 16 clash between England and Mexico in the quarter-finals The post World Cup: Haaland eliminates Brazil, fires Norway into historic quarter-finals appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Mexico vs England live: Hosts cut Three Lions' lead as England go down to 10 men
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Co-hosts Mexico aim to leave home soil with a bang as they take on Harry Kane’s England in their final game at the iconic Azteca Stadium, the scene of Maradona’s infamous handball 40 years ago, with a place in the World Cup quarter-finals up for grabs. Kick-off in Mexico City was delayed by an hour

item.title: Inspired2Lead trains NMDPRA staff on energy transition to strengthen sector regulation
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Inspired2Lead has concluded a five-day capacity-building programme for staff of the Nigerian Midstream and Downstream Petroleum Regulatory Authority (NMDPRA), aimed read more Inspired2Lead trains NMDPRA staff on energy transition to strengthen sector regulation

item.title: Presidency Trains Over 100 Northern Influencers To Counter Fake News
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Office of the Senior Special Assistant to President Bola Tinubu on Media and Public Enlightenment, Mr Abdulaziz Abdulaziz, has trained over 100 social media influencers from across Northern Nigeria on the effective use of social media. Speaking during the training on Sunday in Kano, Abdulaziz sa

item.title: Venezuelan leader marks Independence Day with message of ‘no social unrest’
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Interim President Delcy Rodriguez once again defends her government's handling of the deadly earthquakes on June 24.

item.title: Jonathan Denies Report Of ₦500bn Offer To Split South-South Votes
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former President Goodluck Jonathan has dismissed as false a media report alleging that he was offered ₦500 billion to contest the 2027 presidential election in order to split votes in the South-South region. Jonathan’s Special Adviser on Media, Dr. Ikechukwu Eze, rejected the claim in a statem

item.title: Haaland sends Norway into quarter-finals as Brazil crash out
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Erling Haaland scores twice for Norway as they beat Brazil 2-1 at the New York New Jersey Stadium to reach the quarter-finals of the World Cup for the first time.

item.title: Morocco guaranteed $31.5m in FIFA prize money after quarter-final berth
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: North African giants Morocco have earned a minimum of $31.5 million in total prize money from FIFA after making history read more Morocco guaranteed $31.5m in FIFA prize money after quarter-final berth
